CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2023 Set For Trial On Nov-27-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Robert M. Maltz Esq. for defendant(s) Roman Juarez. Also specially appearance for plaintiff. The jury trial date is continued to 2-26-24 per agreement of parties and there will be no further continuances due to scheduling conflicts. Judge Samuel K. Feng; Clerk VWU; Not Reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion; Motion To Strike Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALEXIS L. DAVIDSON, Esq. of The Michael Law Firm (Tel: 415-447-2833) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ROMAN JUAREZ's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to strike the punitive damages claim is denied.

Plaintiff drove a motor vehicle that hit defendant, a pedestrian on San Francisco's Columbus Avenue. The complaint pleads that plaintiff "fled the scene of the incident without stopping" to render aid and "was aware that fleeing the scene of the incident was a crime." This suffices to support a punitive damage claim at the pleading stage. (See Civil Code 3294.) Punitive damages have been awarded in less egregious motor vehicle cases. (See, e.g., Lakin v. Watkins Associated Industries (1993) 6 Cal.4th 644, 649.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT M. MALTZ, Esq. of Stratman & Williams-Abrego (Tel: 510-457-3440) for defendant Roman Juaraez;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ROMAN JUAREZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant withdraws his motion conceding that service was not properly effected on plaintiff.

The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and orders the matter off calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
